summary: Elder Jorge T. Becerra teaches that paying tithes and offerings helps us overcome double-mindedness, put God first, and receive greater spiritual power and direction.
haiku:
Firstlings humbly given
Heaven’s windows fill with light
God first, hearts made whole
key_points: Putting God first helps disciples avoid the instability of being spiritually double-minded.
Tithing and offerings are modern ways to offer our “firstlings” to the Lord in faith and obedience.
Obedience to the law of tithing brings spiritual illumination, direction, and increased capacity.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Alma 5:12 — the mighty change of heart
Matthew 6:33 — seeking first the kingdom of God
Moses 5:5–9 — Adam and Eve offering the firstlings and receiving the Holy Ghost
President Gordon B. Hinckley’s teaching on tithing and obedience
Elder David A. Bednar, “The Windows of Heaven”
The law of sacrifice and obedience
Putting God first through faith-filled priorities
questions_to_ponder: In what ways might I be spiritually double-minded, and how can I become more single-hearted toward God?
What does it mean in my circumstances to offer the Lord my “firstlings”?
How has paying tithing increased my faith, spiritual capacity, or perspective?
What sacrifices might help me put God first more fully?
How have I seen the “windows of heaven” open in ways beyond financial blessings?
